data.description:
## What

Create `examples/claude-skills/shared/test-design.md` — the shared TDD test-design standard — and wire it into the three skills at the point each incorporates testing. Written in the skills' voice, with the 'scale ceremony to the bug' principle explicit.

The shared file covers:
- Existing-coverage inventory before authoring: the required artifact is a RED oracle, which may be an existing failing test, an existing test strengthened into RED form, or a new test only when nothing covers the behavior. Search by contract wording, public entry point, implementation symbol, error text, and bug/regression identifier; read the nearest tests.
- A test contract card frozen before test code, with scaled fields: contract + citation, bug-triggering preconditions, production path, tier + why lower is insufficient, observable postcondition are always required; negative control, collateral invariants, recovery, fault injection apply conditionally (stateful/atomicity/concurrency work).
- Test-tier selection rule: the smallest tier that still crosses every component in the proven mechanism, with the bug-class -> tier table (unit / producer-consumer contract / real CLI subprocess / real git repos / multi-process with deterministic barrier / parent-child subprocess / kill-and-restart crash recovery / stateful verified fake / behavioral eval).
- 'Trigger the mechanism, assert the contract': arrange conditions that force the mechanism through the smallest production path crossing the faulty seam; assert the public/contractual consequence, never mock call counts, private helpers, intermediate payloads, or source text.
- Minimum behavioral oracle: prove the fixture preconditions; assert an exact behavioral postcondition (not is-not-None / nonempty / no-exception); collateral invariants for stateful operations; a clean retry where the contract is atomicity/recoverability.
- Contrast case: default for input-class defects and stateful operations (nearest passing control / second representative input, parameterized where a family applies); justified omission allowed for trivially single-point bugs.
- Fault realism: the injected fault must occur inside the code whose recovery/cleanup is under test (fail the real second rename, not a top-level wrapper); race tests must synchronize with a barrier/pipe/marker/failpoint, never timing-only sleeps as the primary mechanism.
- Prove the test gates: pytest collects the node; it runs (not SKIP/XFAIL) in the supported CI environment; any temporary xfail is strict=True with a removal condition; the test runs under a gating CI command.
- Defect-seeded mutation: derive the mutation from the confirmed root cause (re-introduce the exact defect), not an arbitrary negation.
- LLM-surface evals: pinned model/settings, paired before/after prompts, multiple samples with a predeclared threshold and reported failure rates, plus a negative control — quantified rates, never one RED and one GREEN sample.

Skill integration (each loads the shared file relative to the skill dir, ../shared/test-design.md):
- `examples/claude-skills/rebar-debug/SKILL.md`: Step 4 becomes 'Design and prove a RED oracle' — inventory first, contract card, tier rule, then RED with proven preconditions; Step 5 mutation check becomes defect-seeded; the fix subagent additionally receives the frozen contract card (intended postcondition + invariants) while fixture/assertions stay held out.
- `examples/claude-skills/rebar-implement/SKILL.md`: Phase 4a/4b reference the shared file for oracle design (inventory, tier, oracle completeness, gating proof); held-out structure unchanged.
- `examples/claude-skills/rebar-brainstorm/SKILL.md`: when recording tickets that include testing work, read the shared file so acceptance criteria name the tier and observable oracle.
- `examples/claude-skills/README.md`: document the shared/ directory convention (copying a single skill requires the shared file too).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `examples/claude-skills/shared/test-design.md` exists and covers every bullet above (inventory, contract card, tier table, trigger/assert rule, oracle minimum, contrast default, fault realism, gating proof, defect-seeded mutation, LLM evals) with the scale-to-the-bug principle stated.
- [ ] All three SKILL.md files reference the shared file at the integration points named above, and rebar-debug's Step 4/5 text is updated as described without weakening the held-out oracle (fixer still never sees test code).
- [ ] `examples/claude-skills/README.md` documents the shared/ convention.
- [ ] `make lint` passes.
data.title: Shared TDD test-design reference (examples/claude-skills/shared/test-design.md) + integrate into the three skills
